The easy idea is to follow the chapter's own clues one by one instead of memorising a loose definition. First notice the words and examples the text repeats: CONSERVATION OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS CONSERVATION OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS, 5.2 Consequences of, 5.1 Deforestation and Its, SCIENCE 54. Then connect each example to the question the chapter is trying to answer. Like solving a source-backed puzzle, every useful point must come from the chapter text itself and stay traceable to the NCERT source.
full_notes:
Coal and Petroleum is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that cONSERVATION OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS CONSERVATION OF PLANTS AND ANIMALS We saw in Class VII that Paheli and Boojho had visited the forest along with Professor Ahmad and Tibu.

The text explains that they were eager to share their experiences with their classmates.

The text explains that other children in the class were also eager to share their experiences as some of them had visited Bharatpur Sanctuary.

The text explains that some others had heard about Kaziranga National Park, Lockchao Wildlife Sanctuary, Great Nicobar Biosphere Reserve and Tiger Reserve, etc.

The text explains that some natural causes of deforestation are forest fires and severe droughts.

The text explains that 5.2 Consequences of Deforestation Paheli and Boojho recalled the consequences of deforestation.

The text explains that they remembered that deforestation increases the temperature and pollution level on the earth.

The text explains that it increases the level of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere.

The text explains that they know that deforestation disturbs the balance in nature.

The text explains that they were told by Professor Ahmad that if cutting of trees continues, rainfall and the fertility of the soil will 5.1 Deforestation and Its Causes A great variety of plants and animals exist on earth.

The text explains that they are essential for the well-being and survival of mankind.

The text explains that today, a major threat to survival of these organisms is deforestation.

The text explains that we know that deforestation means clearing of forests and using that land for other purposes.

The text explains that trees in the forest are cut for some of the purposes mentioned below: l Procuring land for cultivation. l Building houses and factories. l Making furniture or using wood as fuel.

The text explains that how does deforestation reduce rainfall on the one hand and lead to floods on the other? decrease.

The text explains that moreover, there will be increased chances of natural calamities such as floods and droughts.

The text explains that this will lead to global warming as carbon dioxide traps the heat rays reflected by the earth.

The text explains that the increase in temperature on the earth disturbs the water cycle and may reduce rainfall.

The text explains that removal of the top layer of the soil exposes the lower, hard and rocky layers.

The text explains that biosphere is that part of the earth in which living organisms exist or which supports life.

The text explains that biological diversity or biodiversity, refers to the variety of organisms existing on the earth, their interrelationships and their relationship with the environment.

The text explains that he explains that preserving areas of such biological importance make them a part of our national heritage.

The text explains that madhavji explains to the children that apart from our personal efforts and efforts of the society, government To protect our flora and fauna and their habitats, protected areas called wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and biosphere reserves have been earmarked.

The text explains that national Park : Areas reserved for wild life where they can freely use the habitats and natural resources.

The text explains that biosphere Reserve : Large areas of protected land for conservation of wild life, plant and animal resources and traditional life of the tribals living in the area.

The text explains that the government lays down rules, methods and policies to protect and conserve them.

The text explains that madhavji explains that biosphere reserves are the areas meant for conservation of biodiversity.

The text explains that h e explains that animals are comfortable and happy in their own habitat.

The text explains that madhavji explains that some animals and plants typically belong to a particular area.

The text explains that professor Ahmad explains that the destruction of their habitat, increasing population and introduction of new species may affect the natural habitat of endemic species and endanger their existence.

The text explains that professor Ahmad explains that killing (poaching) or capturing animals in general is strictly prohibited and punishable by law in all such places.

The text explains that madhavji tells them that these reserves are large and diverse enough to protect whole sets of ecosystems.

The text explains that can animals live in artificial setting Rock shelters are also found inside the Satpura National Park.

The text explains that a total of 55 rock shelters have been identified in Pachmarhi Biosphere Reserve.

The text explains that madhavji explains that Project Tiger was launched by the government to protect the tigers in the country.

The text explains that at times, we kill snakes, frogs, lizards, bats and owls ruthlessly without realising their importance in the ecosystem.

The text explains that they might be small in size but their role in the ecosystem cannot be ignored.

The text explains that an ecosystem is made of all the plants, animals and microorganisms in an area along with non-living components such as climate, soil, river deltas etc. or collection of firewood, mdicinal plants, etc. are not allowed 2024-25

The text explains that sCIENCE 60 5.9 Red Data Book Professor Ahmad explains about Red Data Book to the children.

The text explains that he tells them that Red Data Book is the source book which keeps a record of all the endangered animals and plants.

The text explains that (a) Wildlife sanctuary and biosphere reserve (b) Zoo and wildlife sanctuary (c) Endangered and extinct species (d) Flora and fauna KEYWORDS KEYWORDS KEYWORDS KEYWORDS KEYWORDS BIODIVERSITY BIOSPHERE RESERVE DEFORESTATION DESERTIFICATION ECOSYSTEM ENDANGERED SPECIES ENDEMIC SPECIES EXTINCT FAUNA FLORA MIGRATORY BIRDS NATIONAL PARK RED DATA BOOK REFORESTATION SANCTUARY 2024-25

The text explains that (a) Wild animals (b) Environment (c) Villages (Rural areas) (d) Cities (Urban areas) (e) Earth (f) The next generation 4.

The text explains that explain how deforestation leads to reduced rainfall.

The text explains that is it justified to prevent tribals from staying in the core area of the forest?

The text explains that make a list of the new information you have gathered from this chapter.

The text explains that karnataka Government had launched ‘Project Elephant’ to save Asian elephants in the state.

The text explains that india contains globally important population of some of Asia’s rarest animals such as the Bengal fox, Marbled cat, Asiatic lion, Indian elephant, Asiatic wild ass, Indian rhinoceros, gaur, Wild asiatic water buffalo, etc.

The text explains that for knowing more, you may contact: l Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Govt. of India Environment, Forest and Wildlife Department Indira Paryavaran Bhavan, Jor Bagh Road, New Delhi -110003 Website: http://envfor.nic.in 3.

The text explains that discuss the matter in your class and note down the points for and against the motion in your notebook.

The text explains that prepare a detailed report with photographs and sketches of the flora and fauna.

The text explains that which information did you find the most interesting and why?

The text explains that do you find any difference between a currency paper and paper of your notebook?

Source: NCERT Class 8 Science, Chapter 5: Coal and Petroleum
